rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. HUNTER MANUFACTURING GROUP,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Osc Re: Contempt Of Judgment By Deft Hunter Manufacturing Group, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Notice Of Motion For Osc Re: Contempt Of Judgment By Deft Hunter Manufacturing Group, Inc. Denied without prejudice. The Court is not satisfied that a contempt proceeding is the proper procedure to enforce this judgment. In any event, The Liang declaration is insufficient to establish all the necessary factual elements. . Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JACK MAMIYE et al VS. MOTION MEDIA NETWORK, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Request For Osc Re: Contempt; Notice Of Motion For Order Imposing Evidence Sanction Against Deft Roark O'Neill And Imposing Monetary Sanction Against Deft For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Continued Deposition; And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Roard O'Neill
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JACK MAMIYE, 1122 HOWARD STREET, LLC's Notice Of Request For OSC Re: Contempt; Notice Of Motion For Order Imposing Evidence Sanction Against Deft Roark O'Neill And Imposing Monetary Sanction Against Deft For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Continued Deposition; And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Roard O'Neill. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear and advise Court of date certain by which defendant ROARK O'NEILL's deposition shall be completed. Amended Order to issue at time of hearing. Request for evidence sanction DENIED. Request for monetary sanctions DENIED. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. 24HR FITNESS CENTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANTS 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC, TYLER HANSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendants shifted the burden to show that there was no discriminatory intent. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact or to offer any admissible evidence to carry his burden of presenting evidence from which a trier of fact could conclude that he would prevail. Contrary to his burden in responding to a demurrer, Plaintiff cannot rely on the allegations in his complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437(c)(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY AUSTIN, TRUSTEE, OF THE AUSTIN FAMILY TRUST et al VS. PATRICE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 9, RESPONDENT PATRICE PHILLIPS' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Denied. Respondent failed to show that the judgment entered against her in this case is void. There is no suggestion that she was not properly served with the Petition to confirm the arbitration award. That confirmation is now a final judgment. Had she had any viable grounds to challenge confirmation of the award, she needed to assert them in opposition to confirmation in this Court. In any event, the evidence supports the conclusion that she was served with the arbitration demand in accordance with the applicable rules, and this Court could not have reviewed a mistake of law even had Respondent timely raised it. Petitioners request to present oral testimony at hearing is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTS KEVIN BORN, ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING AND ENGINEERING'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied. rulings.Case Title: NOEL WEEKS VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NOEL WEEKS' Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff NOEL WEEK's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, is GRANTED. Defendant LINDA CATRON shall serve amended, verified responses to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One, Interrogatory 2.6(a), Interrogatory 2.6(b), and 3.6(c) on or before March 15, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NOEL WEEKS VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Demand To Identify And Produce Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NOEL WEEKS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Demand To Identify And Produce Documents. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff NOEL WEEK's Motion to Compel Responses Request for Production of Documents, Set One, is GRANTED. Defendant LINDA CATRON shall serve amended, verified responses to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Request Nos. 1-4, 59-69 on or before March 15, 2013. Defendant LINDA CATRON shall produce the documents requested in Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Request Nos. 5, 11-15, on or before March 15, 2013. Defendant LINDA CATRON shall serve amended, verified responses to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Request Nos. 16-19, 23-26, 28-31, 34, 37-38 and produce the documents requested to the extent such documents are publicly available, on or before March 15, 2013. Defendant LINDA CATRON shall serve amended, verified responses to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Request Nos. 51-55 and produce the documents, which may be redacted to protect the privacy of any third parties identified, on or before March 15, 2013. The Court will hear argument on all remaining Requests for Production at issue. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DIETER PFEIFER VS. RAINFOREST CAFE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Take Deposition In Foreign Country [Ccp 2027.010, 28 Usc 1781]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT RAINFOREST CAFE, INC., LANDRY'S INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS LANDRY'S Motion To Take Deposition In Foreign Country [CCP 2027.010, 28 Usc 1781]. rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reecuse Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S Motion To Recuse Plaintiff's Counsel Denied. Even assuming some of the testimony of Plaintiffs' counsel may be appropriate, the Court is not inclined to deprive plaintiffs of their chosen counsel in light of their certification under oath in their declarations that they want to keep current counsel notwithstanding the possibility that counsel may become a witness. It will be left to future proceedings to determine what discovery or testimony may properly be elicited from counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Stay The Case
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S Motion To Stay The Case Hearing required. Is the fact that some of the relief sought in this case may be mitigated by relief obtained in the arbitration proceeding sufficient to raise a "possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact" and do the arbitration and this action arise "out of the same transaction or series of related transactions" within the meaning of CCP section 1281.2(c)? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC. VS. CHOUNG MIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Deft'S To Respond To Discovery Requests, The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions And That The Genuineness Of Any Documents And The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Admission Requests Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC.'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants To Respond To Discovery Requests, The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions And That The Genuineness Of Any Documents And The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Admission Requests Be Deemed Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC's Motion for an Order Compelling Defendant to Respond to Discovery Requests is GRANTED. Defendant CHOUNG MIN, ind. & dba MINS DELIVERY SERVICE shall serve verified responses, without objection, the following sets of discovery on or before March 15, 2013: Plaintiff QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC's (1) Form Interrogatories to Defendant, Set One; (2) Requests for Production of Documents to Defendant, Set One; and (3) Requests for Admission to Defendant, Set One. Plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $810.00 is GRANTED, and such amount shall be paid by Defendant CHOUNG MIN, ind. & dba MINS DELIVERY SERVICE on or before March 15, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GREG CONTOS, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. KOKKARI LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 14, 2013, Line 24, DEFENDANTS GEORGE MARCUS, KENNETH FRANGANDAKIS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 2, and 3. Overruled as to cause of action 4. The demurrer was premised solely on there being no cause of action against an employee and the Plaintiff's failure to allege that the individual defendants were his employer. The Court concludes there can be such a claim under appropriate circumstances. The Court does not reach or consider the additional arguments raised in the reply brief concerning the adequacy of the allegations assuming there may be such a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
